# Cluster Profiles

This file records cluster-specific build/run/test instructions discovered over time.
Append new entries rather than rewriting existing ones unless explicitly asked.

## Template

```
### <cluster-name>

Host patterns:
- <hostname or regex>

Module stack:
```bash
module load <modules>
```

## ICL (RDS/EPHEMERAL) — CX3

Host patterns:
- `*.imperial.ac.uk`, e.g. `login-b.cx3.hpc.ic.ac.uk`
- `/rds/general/user/*/home`
- `/rds/general/user/*/ephemeral`

Note: the git repo lives in the `u-dales/` subdirectory (e.g. `~/udales/u-dales`),
not the top-level project folder.

Module stack (self-consistent Intel 2021a — this is what loads reliably as of 2026-07):
```bash
module load tools/prod
module load intel/2021a netCDF/4.8.0-iimpi-2021a netCDF-Fortran/4.5.3-iimpi-2021a \
            FFTW/3.3.9-intel-2021a CMake/3.29.3-GCCcore-13.3.0 git/2.45.1-GCCcore-13.3.0
# -> mpiifort = ifort 2021.2
```
Do NOT use the older documented line
`intel/2025a netCDF/4.9.2-iimpi-2023a ... FFTW/3.3.9-intel-2021a ...`: it mixes three
toolchain years and no longer co-loads on CX3 (Lmod conflict; `intel/2025a cannot be
loaded`). CX3 has no Intel-toolchain netCDF/FFTW newer than 2021a/2023a, so a modern
ifx/2025a full build isn't possible from modules without building netCDF-Fortran from
source. `tools/build_executable.sh icl` was updated to the 2021a stack above.

Solver build:
```bash
./tools/build_executable.sh icl debug
./tools/build_executable.sh icl release
```

Preprocessing build:
```bash
./tools/build_preprocessing.sh icl
```

Python environment:
```bash
# The ~/udales/.venv is a Python 3.9.6 venv (built from Python/3.9.6-GCCcore-11.2.0).
# Load THAT module so libpython3.9.so.1.0 is on LD_LIBRARY_PATH, then activate:
module purge            # the venv is incompatible with the build/other modules
module load Python/3.9.6-GCCcore-11.2.0
source ~/udales/.venv/bin/activate
```
If you skip the matching Python module you get
`libpython3.9.so.1.0: cannot open shared object file` — that's a missing/mismatched
module, NOT a broken venv. Note this Python (GCCcore-11.2.0) differs from the 2021a
build stack, so load it in its own step for the Python/test phase.

MPI launcher notes:
- prefer the module-provided `mpiexec`
- if Open MPI is used, set `TMPDIR=/tmp` and `--oversubscribe` for login-node runs
- interactive runs can be slow; prefer repo wrappers

Gotchas (learned the hard way):
- Shell/module state does NOT persist between separate commands — load modules in the
  same command as the build. `module load … | tail` runs `module` in a subshell and has
  no effect; never pipe a `module load`.
- Stale `.mod` files left in the repo root from old in-source builds shadow `build/debug`
  when compiling from the repo root and cause bogus "interface mismatch / not generated by
  this compiler" errors. `rm -f *.mod *__genmod.f90` in the repo root to clear them.
- Debug builds land in `build/debug` (lowercase, from the `debug` arg), not `build/Debug`.
- `git push` from a plain shell here has no GitHub credential (HTTPS remote, no helper/
  token). Two options: push from VSCode Source Control / a VSCode integrated terminal, or
  use the gh CLI's credential helper without changing config:
  `git -c credential.helper= -c credential.helper='!gh auth git-credential' push origin <branch>`
  (requires `gh auth status` to show a logged-in account).

Tests:
```bash
python tests/run_tests.py supported --branch-a <branch_a> --branch-b <branch_b> --build-type <Debug|Release>
bash tests/integration/mpi_operators/run_test.sh
python tests/integration/processor_boundaries/test_processor_boundaries.py
```

Solver build:
```bash
./tools/build_executable.sh <system> <debug|release>
```

Preprocessing build:
```bash
./tools/build_preprocessing.sh <system>
```

Python environment:
```bash
module load Python/<version>
source /path/to/.venv/bin/activate
```

MPI launcher notes:
- <mpiexec path or required extra args>

Tests:
```bash
python tests/run_tests.py supported --branch-a <branch_a> --branch-b <branch_b> --build-type <Debug|Release>
bash tests/integration/mpi_operators/run_test.sh
```
```
